records.ety: [Cf. G. jü, interj., used in calling to a horse, It. gi, F. dia, used to turn a horse to the left.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Geed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Geeing.]

defs: To turn to the off side, or from the driver (i.e., in the United States, to the right side); -- said of cattle, or a team; used most frequently in the imperative, often with off, by drivers of oxen, in directing their teams, and opposed to haw, or hoi.
notes: ☞ In England, the teamster walks on the right-hand side of the cattle; in the United States, on the left-hand side. In all cases, however, gee means to turn from the driver, and haw to turn toward him.

defs: To cause (a team) to turn to the off side, or from the driver.
